note: PR Freelance pipeline scrape May 1-7, 2026. 6 fresh leads, all 60+ combined. Mix of confirmed contract/freelance postings (Talent Table fintech contract) and strategic agency-overflow targets (Vested, Inkhouse, Reveille — Mark's exact white-label ICP). Heavy fintech/B2B/financial services tilt per Mark's preferences. No duplicates of pr-freelance-scrape-2026-04-30 through 2026-05-06 or any daily-scrape leads. Job-board postings sourced via web search of Google/Bing indexed results — no LinkedIn bot scraping.

leads.name: Talent Table (Fintech / Digital Payments client)
leads.industry: Fintech / Digital Payments — Issues Management & Crisis Comms
leads.fundingStage: Public / Late-stage (anonymous global financial services + digital payments client)
leads.compensationText: $68.97/hr W2 + paid holidays via Talent Table — 12-month contract
leads.employmentTypeRaw: 12-month W2 contract via staffing partner Talent Table

contacts.name: Talent Table Recruiting
contacts.title: Recruiter — Corporate Comms Issues Mgmt role

leads.notes: Highest-intent lead this week (combined 88, A grade). Anonymous global financial services + digital payments client — likely Visa, Mastercard, PayPal, or a top-5 payments network — hiring 12-month contract Corporate Communications Lead via Talent Table staffing. Remit: early-warning systems for reputational risk, cross-functional comms strategy for issues/crisis, sensitive-topic plans, key-point-of-contact during crises. Mark's expertise: crisis comms, reputation management, fintech narrative, regulatory framing — direct match. Intent signals: explicit 12-month duration, $68.97/hr W2 rate stated upfront ($135k annualized base), no H-1B sponsorship (US-only filter), Pacific hours preferred. Pitch: position as senior crisis-comms operator with fintech depth — Mark's edge over staffing-firm contractor pool is strategic + executional combo, not just media-list management. Apply via Talent Table OR identify the underlying payments client and pitch direct.

leads.name: Vested
leads.industry: PR Agency — Financial Services Specialist (Fintech, Wealth Mgmt, InsurTech, Capital Markets, Mortgage, VC, PE)
leads.compensationText: Project + retainer rates negotiable; freelance contractor pool

contacts.name: Vested Talent / Resourcing Lead
contacts.title: Director of Talent or Head of Operations

leads.notes: Tier-1 agency-overflow lead (combined 80, A grade). Vested is a boutique PR firm whose entire client roster sits inside Mark's ICP — fintech, wealth management, insurance/InsurTech, capital markets, mortgage, VC, PE. Mark's exact specialty stack. Boutique size = uses freelance contractor pool for overflow (campaign launches, founder positioning sprints, IPO/exit comms support). Pitch: white-label senior PR support — drop-in capacity for media relations execution + executive comms ghostwriting on their fintech book. No public posting required — this is a relationship-led intro to their resourcing lead. Mark gets sophisticated buyer (agency-side, no client education needed) + recurring overflow flow. Target: founder Dan Simon directly + ops/talent lead via LinkedIn. Send 1-page capabilities deck + 2-3 fintech earned-media samples.

leads.name: Inkhouse
leads.industry: PR Agency — B2B SaaS, Tech, Pre-IPO/IPO Communications
leads.location: Boston, MA + San Francisco, CA + remote

contacts.name: Inkhouse Talent / Resourcing Team
contacts.title: Head of Talent or Managing Director

leads.notes: Strong agency-overflow lead (combined 73, B grade). Inkhouse has supported dozens of company exits including Databricks, ServiceTitan, ForSight Robotics — exactly the B2B SaaS pre-IPO/IPO narrative Mark can plug into. They run high-stakes campaigns that need overflow execution: media list building, analyst pre-briefings, S-1 announcement pushback, post-IPO earnings narrative. Pitch: senior freelance operator who can come in for IPO sprint cycles or campaign-by-campaign. Less natural than Vested (broader sector mix vs. fintech-pure) but volume is larger. Target: Head of Talent or MD on LinkedIn. Reference fintech narrative wins from Mark's portfolio to differentiate from generalist contractor pool.

leads.name: Rho
leads.industry: Fintech / Business Banking — Corporate Cards, Treasury, AP for Startups & Scale-Ups
leads.location: New York, NY + San Francisco, CA
leads.fundingStage: Series B+ (~355 employees as of Jan 2026)
leads.compensationText: Not posted — fractional/contract pitch warranted given team scale and 40+ open reqs
leads.employmentTypeRaw: no posted comms-specific contract — strategic outreach lead

contacts.name: Rho Marketing/Comms Lead
contacts.title: Head of Marketing or VP Brand

leads.notes: Strategic outreach lead (combined 65, B grade). Rho is hiring aggressively (40+ roles loaded) during a 2026 cycle when most peer fintechs are preserving cash — that's an unambiguous strength signal and a comms tailwind opportunity. They sell business banking to startups, so their narrative is B2B SaaS + fintech crossover (Mark's sweet spot). No specific comms-contract posting was visible in indexed results, but companies in this growth posture frequently need fractional comms support to keep pace with product/funding announcements. Pitch: fractional 15-20 hrs/week comms operator for press cycle support, exec positioning (CEO Everett Cook is publicly active), category narrative around 'banking for startups' vs. Mercury/Brex. Target: Head of Marketing or CEO via LinkedIn cold outreach with portfolio examples in fintech B2B.

leads.name: Kaia Investment Partners
leads.industry: Venture Capital — Stablecoin & Fintech Ecosystem Fund
leads.location: Asia-Pacific HQ (likely Seoul/Singapore) — remote engagement viable
leads.compensationText: Not posted — fund launch comms project pitch warranted
leads.employmentTypeRaw: no posted role — greenfield comms outreach

contacts.name: Kaia Investment Partners — Founding Partner
contacts.title: Managing Partner / Head of Investor Relations

leads.notes: Greenfield comms lead (combined 63, B grade). Kaia Investment Partners launched March 2026 as the investment arm of Kaia, with a dedicated VC fund focused on stablecoin and fintech ecosystems. New funds in stablecoin/crypto-adjacent verticals have very specific comms needs: fund-launch announcement, founding-partner thought leadership, regulatory positioning (stablecoin policy is a 2026 hot button), LP visibility, portfolio company narrative coordination. No posted comms role — this is a strategic outreach pitch. Mark's edge: senior PR operator who has worked the fintech + alternative-investments narrative before, can build and run a comms function fractionally during the first 12-18 months while the fund decides whether to staff full-time. Lower combined score than Vested/Inkhouse because no posted contract, but high recency (March 2026 launch = current need).

leads.name: Reveille Advisors
leads.industry: PR Agency — Earned Media & Reputation for Public Companies, PE, Funds
leads.notes: Agency-overflow lead (combined 60, B grade). Reveille Advisors is a boutique earned-media + reputation PR shop serving public companies, PE-backed firms, and funds — overlapping cleanly with Mark's ICP (alternative investments + capital markets). Smaller than Inkhouse, narrower than Vested, but the earned-media remit is specifically Mark's wheelhouse. Boutiques of this scale almost always use freelance contractors during peak cycles. Pitch: white-label senior earned-media operator with public-company + fund-side experience. Target: managing partner via LinkedIn with sample placements in WSJ/Bloomberg/FT/Barron's. Lowest combined score in this scrape but threshold-clearing — include because Mark's emphasis on agency overflow makes boutique-PE-focused shops strategically valuable even when not actively posting.
